rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORISA TURNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/15/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Notice was mailed to Shawn A. Turner at an address that is different from the address shown in the petition. (4) A proof of service, filed on 7/2/25, shows a different address for Javon C. White from the address shown in the petition, but a copy of the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 Notice of Hearing was not attached to that pleading form of proof of service and nothing has been filed to indicate that his address is different from the one set forth in the petition. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/1/15. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The decedent was reported to have died on 10/1/20, not 10/10/20.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL J. TEDESCHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/15/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "First Supplement" filed on 7/2/25 AMENDED the petition. [See CRC 7.3.] (2) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (3) Madelane Dixon's current address was not set forth in any verified pleading. (4) The new Exhibit C attached to the "First Supplement" filed on 7/2/25 shows 954.78 shares of SIT New Beginning United St, but there were 9,554.78 shares inventoried and appraised at $107,969.01 (5) There were 63.48, not 61.4160, share of Invesco International Equit inventoried. (6) The 43.139 units of AB Global Bond Fund Inc Adv were appraised at $353.74, not $300.25. (7) There were 47.685, not 47.785, shares of American Century Internatio inventoried. (8) None of the holdings listed on the final page of Exhibit D to the "First Supplement" were on the I&A filed on 1/27/25. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/1/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TINA MEI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/25. The following issues to be cleared two weeks before: (1) Disclaimer is irrevocable and not an assignment, disposing of the disclaimed interests as if the disclaimant predeceased the decedent. Prob. Code sections. 281. Petitioner alleges that Ms. A. Mei has two children who are entitled to her share as a result of the disclaimer. It appears that the petition needs to be properly amended to mirror the said proposed distribution (to Ms. A. Mei's issues, considering one issue is a minor), or the petitioner needs to provide an authority under which the court can grant the proposed distribution of Ms. A. Mei's shares to the petitioner, not her children. (2) The date of birth of the minor child of Ms. A. Mei. (3) The children of the disclaimant must be properly noticed. (4) Assets included in the Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) must be identified to ensure that all accounts are accurately listed on Attachment 1. If any account contains securities held in a brokerage account, these must be appraised by the probate referee. Listing merely financial institutions' names are insufficient. In such cases, a new probate referee must be appointed, and a corrected Inventory and Appraisal must be submitted. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRANK S.Y. CHIN REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Some of the issues to be addressed: 1) No legal authority is cited in the petition aside from the caption. No pour-over will has been submitted either. 2) The Grant Deed recorded on 4/27/01 granted a 1/2 interest to Frank S.Y. Chin and Jean Y. Chin, husband and wife, as tenants in common, not joint tenants, as alleged in paragraph 4 of the petition. 3) A copy of a supplement must be served if a copy of the pleading was required (as with Probate Code Section 850 petitions). [CRC 7.53(b).] 4) The case number is incomplete in the caption of the supplement filed on 6/16/25 and the notices of hearing filed on 6/16/25 and 7/17/25. 5) It appears that the decedent's interest in the real property was more than 25%. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY A.L. CHUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 6/6/16. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status hearing will be set for 3/2/27. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before that status hearing date, no appearance will be required on that date. (The Court will waive the minor defects in the petition. Item 3g(1)(d) of the petition is supposed to be checked when other named executors will not act because of death or declination or some other reason. The first named executor, Burk H. Lim, is deceased. Also, the trust beneficiaries are not specifically identified as such in the "Supplemental Declaration in Response to Probate Examiner's Comments" filed on 7/23/25. NB: A supplement or an amendment to a petition should stand on its own and not "respond" to issues raised by the court.)
